verses.text: The Pharisees fault the disciples for breaking 'the tradition of the elders'; He replies, why break God's commandment by your tradition? Misplaced zeal. They guard man's rules and trample God's law.
The Pharisees fault the disciples for breaking 'the tradition of the elders'; He replies, why break God's commandment by your tradition? Misplaced zeal. They guard man's rules and trample God's law.
The Pharisees fault the disciples for breaking 'the tradition of the elders'; He replies, why break God's commandment by your tradition? Misplaced zeal. They guard man's rules and trample God's law.
God commands honour to parents, but they let a 'gift' to the temple excuse neglect, 'making the commandment of God of none effect.' Hollow piety. Religious excuses cannot cancel plain duty.
God commands honour to parents, but they let a 'gift' to the temple excuse neglect, 'making the commandment of God of none effect.' Hollow piety. Religious excuses cannot cancel plain duty.
God commands honour to parents, but they let a 'gift' to the temple excuse neglect, 'making the commandment of God of none effect.' Hollow piety. Religious excuses cannot cancel plain duty.
Well did Isaiah say, 'this people... honoureth me with their lips, but their heart is far from me.' Empty worship. God is not deceived by words without the heart.
Well did Isaiah say, 'this people... honoureth me with their lips, but their heart is far from me.' Empty worship. God is not deceived by words without the heart.
Well did Isaiah say, 'this people... honoureth me with their lips, but their heart is far from me.' Empty worship. God is not deceived by words without the heart.
Not what enters the mouth defiles a man, but what comes out of it. The real defilement. Sin is a matter of the heart, not of the diet.
Not what enters the mouth defiles a man, but what comes out of it. The real defilement. Sin is a matter of the heart, not of the diet.
The offended Pharisees are 'blind leaders of the blind'; both shall fall into the ditch. Dangerous guides. To follow blind teachers is to share their ruin.
The offended Pharisees are 'blind leaders of the blind'; both shall fall into the ditch. Dangerous guides. To follow blind teachers is to share their ruin.
The offended Pharisees are 'blind leaders of the blind'; both shall fall into the ditch. Dangerous guides. To follow blind teachers is to share their ruin.
What enters the mouth passes away; it cannot defile the soul. Outward things. Bodily food does not touch the inner man.
What enters the mouth passes away; it cannot defile the soul. Outward things. Bodily food does not touch the inner man.
What enters the mouth passes away; it cannot defile the soul. Outward things. Bodily food does not touch the inner man.
But from the heart come evil thoughts, murders, and adulteries; these defile. The heart's overflow. Defilement springs from within, not from unwashed hands.
But from the heart come evil thoughts, murders, and adulteries; these defile. The heart's overflow. Defilement springs from within, not from unwashed hands.
But from the heart come evil thoughts, murders, and adulteries; these defile. The heart's overflow. Defilement springs from within, not from unwashed hands.
A Canaanite woman cries for her vexed daughter; He answers not a word, and the disciples would send her away. Silence tested. Her faith is tried by seeming refusal.
A Canaanite woman cries for her vexed daughter; He answers not a word, and the disciples would send her away. Silence tested. Her faith is tried by seeming refusal.
A Canaanite woman cries for her vexed daughter; He answers not a word, and the disciples would send her away. Silence tested. Her faith is tried by seeming refusal.
He says He is sent to Israel's lost sheep, and it is not right to give the children's bread to dogs. A hard word. Yet it draws out, not crushes, her faith.
He says He is sent to Israel's lost sheep, and it is not right to give the children's bread to dogs. A hard word. Yet it draws out, not crushes, her faith.
He says He is sent to Israel's lost sheep, and it is not right to give the children's bread to dogs. A hard word. Yet it draws out, not crushes, her faith.
'Truth, Lord: yet the dogs eat of the crumbs'; 'O woman, great is thy faith,' and her daughter is healed. Persistent faith. Humble, clinging trust wins from Christ what it seeks.
'Truth, Lord: yet the dogs eat of the crumbs'; 'O woman, great is thy faith,' and her daughter is healed. Persistent faith. Humble, clinging trust wins from Christ what it seeks.
He heals the lame, blind, dumb, and maimed; the crowd glorifies the God of Israel. Wondering praise. His mercies move the multitude to worship.
He heals the lame, blind, dumb, and maimed; the crowd glorifies the God of Israel. Wondering praise. His mercies move the multitude to worship.
He heals the lame, blind, dumb, and maimed; the crowd glorifies the God of Israel. Wondering praise. His mercies move the multitude to worship.
Moved with compassion for the hungry crowd, He finds seven loaves and a few fishes. Renewed pity. He will not send the faint away unfed.
Moved with compassion for the hungry crowd, He finds seven loaves and a few fishes. Renewed pity. He will not send the faint away unfed.
Moved with compassion for the hungry crowd, He finds seven loaves and a few fishes. Renewed pity. He will not send the faint away unfed.
He feeds four thousand, and seven baskets remain. Abundant grace. Christ's supply is never exhausted by the need before Him.
He feeds four thousand, and seven baskets remain. Abundant grace. Christ's supply is never exhausted by the need before Him.
